_patch_meta.description: vcb-010 re-evaluation — Session D cross-registry pointers. One pointer raised: SD_POINTER for apeitheō (G0544) connecting registry 165 (unbelief) to registry 50 (disobedient) via the cognate XREF G0545 apeithēs. Companion VCSBFLAGS patch carries the within-registry PH2_BOUNDARY_QUESTION for the same term.

record.flag_label: apeitheō (G0544) connects registry 165 unbelief to registry 50 disobedient
record.description: Cross-registry pointer raised during vcb-010 verse-context re-evaluation of apeitheō (G0544, mti=1199) in registry 165 (unbelief). The Session A XREF table for this term lists G0545 apeithēs (disobedient) as OWNER in registry 50. STEP/Mounce: the verb does not separate 'disbelieve' and 'disobey' the way English does — the same verb expresses both. Five characteristic-distinct groups emerged in vcb-010: two clusters around refusal-of-Christ-revelation and active-hostility (1199-001, 1199-002); three around moral-truth-refusal, covenantal-disobedience, and Pauline-mercy-logic-disobedience (1199-003, 1199-004, 1199-005). Session D implication: the synthesis between registry 165 (unbelief) and registry 50 (disobedient) needs an explicit account of how the same Greek verb is distributed across two semantic-domain registries — and whether the registry-50 OWNER analysis of G0545 apeithēs informs (or is informed by) the registry-165 treatment of G0544 apeitheō. The five-group structure for G0544 in 165 already exposes the two halves of the verb's semantic range — Session D may consider this when integrating the two registries' findings.
